pattern: Long division builds the decimal digit by digit, and remembering where each remainder first appeared marks exactly where the repeating cycle begins.
hints: Handle the sign once up front, then work with magnitudes; the integer part is plain division.
Continue dividing the remainder by ten as in long division; a remainder you have seen before means the digits since then repeat forever.
Record each remainder's position in the fractional string; when a remainder repeats, wrap everything from its first position to the end in parentheses.

edge_inputs.description: both negative cancel to positive repeating

stress.description: random numerator and denominator pairs spanning signs and magnitudes
stress.note: denominator never zero; one pair per generated test
